Global (All).title: 'Russia warns Finland and Sweden against NATO membership'
Global (All).summary: Russia warned Finland and Sweden against joining NATO, and reiterated that any such move would harm bilateral relations.

Global (All).title: 'Russia launches new wave of attacks on Ukrainian cities'
Global (All).summary: Russia unleashed a new wave of attacks on Ukrainian cities, including Kyiv, Kharkiv, and Mariupol, as the war rages on.

Global (All).title: 'G7 announces new sanctions on Russia over Ukraine conflict'
Global (All).summary: The G7 countries announced new sanctions on Russia, targeting its economy and individuals involved in the Ukraine conflict.

United States.title: Gunman kills two people at California shopping center
United States.summary: Two people were killed in a shooting at a shopping center in California. The gunman remains at large.

United States.title: Supreme Court overturns Roe v. Wade, restricting abortion access
United States.summary: The US Supreme Court has overturned the landmark Roe v. Wade decision, potentially restricting access to abortion nationwide.

United States.title: US Supreme Court rules against Biden administration in immigration case
United States.summary: The US Supreme Court has ruled against the Biden administration in a case involving the administration's authority to end the Trump-era 'Remain in Mexico' immigration policy.

South Korea.title: North Korea conducts missile test: Report claims 'unidentified projectiles'
South Korea.summary: South Korea detected the launch and reported that two unidentified projectiles were fired by North Korea on June 20, 2024. The launch reportedly originated from the North Korean coastal city of Wonsan.

South Korea.title: South Korea strengthens military presence near Demilitarized Zone after North Korea’s alleged border incursion
South Korea.summary: Tensions between South and North Korea increased on June 15, 2024, after the North allegedly crossed into the Demilitarized Zone (DMZ), prompting Seoul to bolster its military presence.

South Korea.title: S. Korea, US conduct joint military drills despite North Korea’s warnings
South Korea.summary: The joint US-South Korea military exercises began on June 18, 2024, prompting a strong objection from North Korea, which sees the drills as a provocation.

Japan.title: Taiwan, Japan Agree on Joint Declaration on Defense Cooperation
Japan.summary: On June 16, 2023, President of Taiwan Tsai Ing-wen had a phone call with Japanese Prime Minister Fumio Kishida, and they agreed on a joint declaration on defense cooperation, further deepening the Taiwan-Japan relationship.

Japan.title: Japan's Government to Approve Record 2.9 Trillion Yen Extra Budget for Military
Japan.summary: The Japanese government plans to approve a record 2.9 trillion yen ($26.6 billion) extra budget for the country's military in the coming weeks to fund procurement, personnel, and other expenses, in light of China's military expansion.

Japan.title: US Secretary of State Blinken to Visit Japan and South Korea to Strengthen Ties
Japan.summary: US Secretary of State Antony Blinken will visit Japan, South Korea, and Australia to strengthen ties and counterbalance China's growing power in the region starting from July, amid increased concerns over the Taiwan Strait tensions.
